response.justification: While the candidate is a highly accomplished software engineer with 30+ years of experience and demonstrated performance optimization expertise, this role fundamentally requires SoC and IP modeling expertise, GPU architecture knowledge, and hardware-software co-design experience. The candidate's entire background is in software development—web, mobile, backend, and recently AI agent infrastructure—with no evidence of hardware design, chip modeling, or AI accelerator architecture work. This represents a categorical mismatch between the candidate's qualifications and the principal hardware architect role.
strengths: Extensive performance optimization experience with distributed systems, microservices, and low-latency applications
Current work in AI agent infrastructure and frameworks shows awareness of AI workload patterns
Demonstrated ability to work across multiple technical domains with strong software engineering fundamentals
concerns: Zero experience with SoC, IP modeling, or chip design—the core responsibility of the role
No background in GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD systems, or hardware-level AI accelerator optimization
Bachelor's degree only with no advanced degree; lacks the typical educational foundation for principal hardware architecture roles

key_factors.factor: GPU and AI accelerator architecture knowledge
